simple_types.name: PSSI_CR_CKPOL_Field
simple_types.code:
type PSSI_CR_CKPOL_Field is
   B_0x0,
   B_0x1)
  with Size => 1;

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x0
enumeration_literals.description: Rising edge active for inputs or falling edge active for outputs.

simple_types.name: PSSI_CR_DEPOL_Field
simple_types.code:
type PSSI_CR_DEPOL_Field is
   B_0x0,
   B_0x1)
  with Size => 1;

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x0
enumeration_literals.description: PSSI_DE active high (1 indicates that data is valid)

simple_types.name: PSSI_CR_DERDYCFG_Field
simple_types.code:
type PSSI_CR_DERDYCFG_Field is
   B_0x0,
   B_0x1,
   B_0x2,
   B_0x3,
   B_0x4,
   B_0x5,
   B_0x6,
   B_0x7)
  with Size => 3;

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x0
enumeration_literals.description: Only PSSI_RDY enabled

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x1
enumeration_literals.description: Only PSSI_DE enabled

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x2
enumeration_literals.description: Both PSSI_RDY and PSSI_DE alternate functions enabled

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x3
enumeration_literals.description:
Both PSSI_RDY and PSSI_DE features enabled - bidirectional on PSSI_RDY pin
(see )

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x4
enumeration_literals.description: Only PSSI_RDY function enabled, but mapped to PSSI_DE pin

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x5
enumeration_literals.description: Only PSSI_DE function enabled, but mapped to PSSI_RDY pin

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x6
enumeration_literals.description:
Both PSSI_RDY and PSSI_DE features enabled - bidirectional on PSSI_DE pin
(see )

simple_types.name: PSSI_CR_DMAEN_Field
simple_types.code:
type PSSI_CR_DMAEN_Field is
   B_0x0,
   B_0x1)
  with Size => 1;

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x0
enumeration_literals.description:
DMA transfers are enabled (default configuration). A DMA channel in the
general-purpose DMA controller must be configured to perform transfers
from/to PSSI_DR.

simple_types.name: PSSI_CR_EDM_Field
simple_types.code:
type PSSI_CR_EDM_Field is
   B_0x0,
   B_0x1,
   B_0x2,
   B_0x3)
  with Size => 2;

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x0
enumeration_literals.description: Reserved, must not be selected

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x1
enumeration_literals.description: Reserved, must not be selected

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x2
enumeration_literals.description: The interface captures 16-bit data on every parallel data clock

simple_types.name: PSSI_CR_ENABLE_Field
simple_types.code:
type PSSI_CR_ENABLE_Field is
   B_0x0,
   B_0x1)
  with Size => 1;

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x0
enumeration_literals.description: PSSI enabled

simple_types.name: PSSI_CR_OUTEN_Field
simple_types.code:
type PSSI_CR_OUTEN_Field is
   B_0x0,
   B_0x1)
  with Size => 1;

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x0
enumeration_literals.description: Transmit mode: data is output synchronously with PSSI_PDCK

simple_types.name: PSSI_CR_RDYPOL_Field
simple_types.code:
type PSSI_CR_RDYPOL_Field is
   B_0x0,
   B_0x1)
  with Size => 1;

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x0
enumeration_literals.description: PSSI_RDY active high (1 indicates that the receiver is ready to receive)

simple_types.name: PSSI_IER_OVR_IE_Field
simple_types.code:
type PSSI_IER_OVR_IE_Field is
   B_0x0,
   B_0x1)
  with Size => 1;

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x0
enumeration_literals.description:
An interrupt is generated if either an overrun or an underrun error
occurred.

simple_types.name: PSSI_MIS_OVR_MIS_Field
simple_types.code:
type PSSI_MIS_OVR_MIS_Field is
   B_0x0,
   B_0x1)
  with Size => 1;

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x0
enumeration_literals.description:
An interrupt is generated if there is either an overrun or an underrun
error and the OVR_IE bit is set in PSSI_IER.

simple_types.name: PSSI_RIS_OVR_RIS_Field
simple_types.code:
type PSSI_RIS_OVR_RIS_Field is
   B_0x0,
   B_0x1)
  with Size => 1;

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x0
enumeration_literals.description:
An overrun/underrun occurred: overrun in receive mode, underrun in transmit
mode.

simple_types.name: PSSI_SR_RTT1B_Field
simple_types.code:
type PSSI_SR_RTT1B_Field is
   B_0x0,
   B_0x1)
  with Size => 1;

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x0
enumeration_literals.description:
FIFO is ready for a one byte (32-bit) transfer. In receive mode, this means
that at least one valid data byte is in the FIFO. In transmit mode, this
means that there is at least one byte free in the FIFO.

simple_types.name: PSSI_SR_RTT4B_Field
simple_types.code:
type PSSI_SR_RTT4B_Field is
   B_0x0,
   B_0x1)
  with Size => 1;

enumeration_literals.name: B_0x0
enumeration_literals.description:
FIFO is ready for a four-byte (32-bit) transfer. In receive mode, this
means that at least four valid data bytes are in the FIFO. In transmit
mode, this means that there are at least four bytes free in the FIFO.

array_types.name: PSSI_DR_BYTE_Field_Array
array_types.code:
type PSSI_DR_BYTE_Field_Array is array (0 .. 3) of PSSI_DR_BYTE_Element
  with Component_Size => 8, Size => 32;

record_types.name: PSSI_CR_Register

fields.name: Reserved_0_4
fields.description:
Parallel data clock polarity This bit configures the capture edge of
the parallel clock or the edge used for driving outputs, depending on
OUTEN.

fields.name: CKPOL
fields.description:
Data enable (PSSI_DE) polarity This bit indicates the level on the
PSSI_DE pin when the data are not valid on the parallel interface.

fields.name: Reserved_7_7
fields.description:
Ready (PSSI_RDY) polarity This bit indicates the level on the
PSSI_RDY pin when the data are not valid on the parallel interface.

fields.name: Reserved_12_13
fields.description:
PSSI enable The contents of the FIFO are flushed when ENABLE is
cleared to 0. Note: When ENABLE=1, the content of PSSI_CR must not be
changed, except for the ENABLE bit itself. All configuration bits can
change as soon as ENABLE changes from 0 to 1. The DMA controller and
all PSSI configuration registers must be programmed correctly before
setting the ENABLE bit to 1. The ENABLE bit and the DCMI ENABLE bit
(bit 15 of DCMI_CR) must not be set to 1 at the same time.

fields.name: Reserved_15_17
fields.description:
Data enable and ready configuration When the PSSI_RDY function is
mapped to the PSSI_DE pin (settings 101 or 111), it is still the
RDYPOL bit which determines its polarity. Similarly, when the PSSI_DE
function is mapped to the PSSI_RDY pin (settings 110 or 111), it is
still the DEPOL bit which determines its polarity.

record_types.name: PSSI_DR_Register
PSSI_ICR_Register

fields.name: Reserved_0_0
fields.description:
Write-only. Data buffer overrun/underrun interrupt status clear
Writing this bit to 1 clears the OVR_RIS bit in PSSI_RIS.

record_types.name: PSSI_IER_Register
PSSI_MIS_Register

fields.name: Reserved_0_0
fields.description:
Read-only. Data buffer overrun/underrun masked interrupt status This
bit is set to 1 only when PSSI_IER/OVR_IE and PSSI_RIS/OVR_RIS are
both set to 1.

record_types.name: PSSI_Peripheral
PSSI_RIS_Register

fields.name: Reserved_0_0
fields.description:
Read-only. Data buffer overrun/underrun raw interrupt status This bit
is cleared by writing a 1 to the OVR_ISC bit in PSSI_ICR.

record_types.name: PSSI_SR_Register

fields.name: Reserved_0_1
fields.description: Read-only. FIFO is ready to transfer four bytes

fields.name: RTT4B
fields.description: Read-only. FIFO is ready to transfer one byte

variables.name: PSSI_Periph
variables.code:
PSSI_Periph : aliased PSSI_Peripheral
  with Import, Address => PSSI_Base;
